content: Linear Temporal Logic Reward Machines (LTL-RMs) can serve as a symbolic indexing backbone for episodic memory in neural episodic planning agents, structuring unstructured stored experience traces by the high-level temporal task constraints they satisfy. This allows neural agents to rapidly retrieve only relevant episodic trajectories that match the current task's logical requirements, rather than brute-force searching all stored memories.
source_topics: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
